name: Visible dark photon resonance searches at e+e- colliders
observable: sigma(e+e- -> gamma A_prime) * BR(A_prime -> e+e-, mu+mu-) and prompt lifetime
required_observables: epsilon; A_prime visible partial widths; total width; hadronic R treatment; detector lifetime regime
missing_observables: epsilon; visible branching fractions; A_prime lifetime; production rate normalization
tested_subregion: full 1sigma m_Aprime box overlaps, but no epsilon/BR is available
source_title: Search for a dark photon in e+e- collisions at BABAR
applicability_notes: Do not exclude from mass alone; if epsilon and BRs are supplied this becomes a direct recast/application.

name: LHCb prompt and displaced dimuon dark photon searches
mass_range: m_Aprime = 0.2806-0.8462 GeV; prompt search overlaps, displaced published low-mass window partly overlaps 0.2806-0.350 GeV
observable: pp -> A_prime + X, A_prime -> mu+mu- prompt/displaced yield
required_observables: epsilon; A_prime lifetime; BR(A_prime -> mu+mu-); production mode mapping
missing_observables: epsilon; muonic branching fraction; lifetime; LHCb acceptance recast
tested_subregion: full mass box for prompt-like search; lower part for displaced window
source_title: Search for dark photons produced in 13 TeV pp collisions
applicability_notes: Requires lifetime and branching information; no exclusion is claimed.

name: Visible fixed-target and beam-dump dark photon searches
mass_range: m_Aprime = 0.2806-0.8462 GeV, overlapping parts of fixed-target/dump compilations
observable: production in electron/proton dump or fixed target times visible decay probability
required_observables: epsilon; lifetime; visible BRs; production rates in target material; shielding/geometry acceptance
missing_observables: epsilon; A_prime total/partial widths; experiment-specific recast inputs
tested_subregion: mass overlap only; coupling/lifetime slice unavailable
applicability_notes: DarkCast-style recast is the appropriate backend once epsilon and decay tables exist.

name: Invisible dark photon missing-energy searches
mass_range: m_Aprime = 0.2806-0.8462 GeV overlaps BaBar invisible range m_Aprime <= 8 GeV and sub-GeV missing-energy programs
coupling_range: epsilon and invisible branching ratio not specified
required_observables: epsilon; invisible final states; BR(A_prime -> invisible); total width; detector acceptance
missing_observables: existence and rate of invisible decay channels; epsilon; lifetime
tested_subregion: mass overlap, but minimal no-fermion model has no specified dark matter invisible channel
source_title: Search for Invisible Decays of a Dark Photon Produced in e+e- Collisions at BaBar
applicability_notes: Not directly applicable unless additional invisible states or long-lived dark-sector final states are defined.

name: Rare meson and resonance production of visible dark photons
mass_range: m_Aprime = 0.2806-0.8462 GeV; pi0 channel closed, eta/vector-meson and heavy-flavor channels may overlap
observable: BR(meson -> gamma A_prime or X A_prime) * BR(A_prime -> SM)
required_observables: epsilon; visible BRs; hadronic decay model; meson production rates; lifetime acceptance
missing_observables: epsilon; A_prime decay table; meson production and acceptance recast
tested_subregion: eta/vector/heavy-flavor overlap only; no coupling slice
source_title: US Cosmic Visions: New Ideas in Dark Matter 2017: Community Report
applicability_notes: Mass alone is insufficient; pi0-specific limits do not overlap because m_Aprime exceeds m_pi0.

name: Light dark-Higgs scalar beam-dump and rare-meson searches
mass_range: m_rho = 0.0286-0.0947 GeV overlaps light scalar ranges below kaon and B masses
coupling_range: lambda_HP or scalar-Higgs mixing angle not specified
observable: production of scalar through Higgs mixing and visible displaced decays
required_observables: lambda_HP; scalar-Higgs mixing angle; rho decay widths/BRs; meson production rates; lifetime acceptance
missing_observables: lambda_HP; mixing angle; rho lifetime; visible branching fractions
source_title: Cosmological beam dump: constraints on dark scalars mixed with the Higgs boson
applicability_notes: Laboratory scalar limits need the portal-induced mixing and lifetime; not applied.

name: Higgs exotic and invisible decays to dark-sector particles
mass_range: m_rho and m_Aprime below m_h/2; kinematically open if portal operators generate h -> rho rho or h -> A_prime A_prime
observable: BR(h -> dark sector), visible/invisible/long-lived final-state branching fractions
required_observables: lambda_HP; scalar mixing; h-rho-rho coupling; h-A_prime-A_prime effective rate; dark-sector decay table
available_observables: dark masses and portal operators listed upstream
missing_observables: lambda_HP; Higgs mixing; exotic Higgs branching fractions; decay lengths
tested_subregion: entire 1sigma region kinematically open, but rate unknown
source_title: Feebly Interacting Particles: FIPs 2022 workshop report
applicability_notes: Kinematics alone does not imply a bound; rates are portal controlled.

name: BBN/CMB constraints on dark photon freeze-in and decays
mass_range: m_Aprime = 0.2806-0.8462 GeV lies within 1 MeV-100 GeV range discussed for decoupled mediators
observable: freeze-in abundance, lifetime, visible/invisible branching, injected electromagnetic/hadronic energy
required_observables: epsilon; A_prime lifetime; freeze-in yield; branching fractions; dark/visible temperature ratio
available_observables: m_Aprime; upstream assumes xi=1 for FOPT
missing_observables: epsilon; abundance/yield; lifetime; branching fractions; BBN/CMB energy-injection observable
tested_subregion: full 1sigma mass range overlaps mediator-decay cosmology studies
source_title: Cosmological Constraints on Decoupled Dark Photons and Dark Higgs
applicability_notes: Cannot apply from lifetime or mass alone; abundance and decay channels are required.

name: BBN/CMB constraints on dark-Higgs freeze-in and decays
mass_range: m_rho = 0.0286-0.0947 GeV lies in scalar ranges covered by cosmological beam-dump analyses
coupling_range: lambda_HP or scalar-Higgs mixing angle not specified
observable: scalar freeze-in yield, lifetime, decay modes, electromagnetic/hadronic energy injection
required_observables: lambda_HP; scalar-Higgs mixing; rho lifetime; decay branching fractions; freeze-in abundance
missing_observables: lambda_HP; mixing angle; rho decay table; freeze-in yield; BBN/CMB likelihood observable
source_title: Cosmological beam dump: constraints on dark scalars mixed with the Higgs boson
applicability_notes: Potentially important because m_rho < 2 m_Aprime, so dark-sector decays may be portal suppressed.

name: Delta N_eff and dark-radiation thermal history
mass_range: masses nonrelativistic by BBN if sector cools conventionally, but abundance/decoupling history unknown
coupling_range: epsilon, lambda_HP, xi history not specified
observable: Delta N_eff and entropy/energy density in dark sector at BBN/CMB
required_observables: thermalization/decoupling temperature; temperature ratio; annihilation/decay history; lifetimes; branching fractions
available_observables: FOPT/PTA assumes xi=1 at transition; masses available
missing_observables: xi evolution to BBN/CMB; dark entropy transfer; decay/annihilation rates; Delta N_eff
tested_subregion: entire 1sigma region; no validated BBN/CMB thermal history
source_title: NANOGrav Signal from a Dark Conformal Phase Transition
applicability_notes: The FOPT xi=1 assumption is not itself a BBN/CMB calculation.

name: Consistency of xi = 1 with negligible portals
mass_range: FOPT temperatures in PTA benchmarks are O(0.01-0.1) GeV; masses are sub-GeV
coupling_range: epsilon and lambda_HP unspecified/negligible for FOPT
observable: interaction rate Gamma_SM-dark / H before and during the transition
required_observables: epsilon; lambda_HP; scattering/annihilation rates; initial-condition assumption; H(T)
available_observables: FOPT/PTA uses xi=1; portals assumed negligible for FOPT
missing_observables: thermalization rate; decoupling temperature; temperature-ratio evolution
tested_subregion: full 1sigma region; requires posterior-level thermal rates
source_title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector
applicability_notes: This is a consistency condition, not an exclusion, until portal values or initial conditions are specified.

name: SN1987A dark photon energy-loss and trapping bounds
mass_range: m_Aprime = 0.2806-0.8462 GeV is above the strongest low-mass SN1987A region; lower edge may need a dedicated check
observable: supernova production, absorption/trapping, and energy loss in core
required_observables: epsilon; in-medium production rate; absorption opacity; branching/decay length; supernova core model
missing_observables: epsilon; supernova in-medium rate and trapping calculation
tested_subregion: lower edge near 0.28 GeV only plausibly overlaps; most of box likely Boltzmann suppressed
source_title: Updated Constraints on Self-Interacting Dark Matter from Supernova 1987A
applicability_notes: Classified approximate because source metadata indicates large astrophysical/systematic uncertainties and lower-mass focus; no quantitative exclusion applied.

name: Ordinary stellar-cooling constraints
mass_range: m_Aprime = 0.2806-0.8462 GeV and m_rho = 0.0286-0.0947 GeV are far above keV stellar core temperatures for standard stellar cooling
required_observables: light enough mediator and couplings to stellar plasma
missing_observables: none for standard keV-temperature stellar cooling
tested_subregion: no thermal overlap with ordinary stellar cores
source_title: Feebly Interacting Particles: FIPs 2022 workshop report
applicability_notes: Boltzmann suppression makes standard star cooling inapplicable; supernova is treated separately.

name: Direct-detection scattering constraints
mass_range: sub-GeV mediators present, but no stable dark matter particle or relic abundance is specified
coupling_range: epsilon/lambda_HP and DM coupling not specified
observable: DM-nucleus/electron scattering cross section or dark photon absorption rate
required_observables: stable DM candidate; local density fraction; scattering/absorption cross section
available_observables: no dark matter particle in validated minimal model
missing_observables: DM identity; relic abundance; local fraction; scattering cross section
tested_subregion: not applicable to minimal no-fermion model as a DM model
source_title: Direct Detection Constraints on Dark Photon Dark Matter
applicability_notes: If rho is promoted to metastable DM, this must be reopened with relic abundance and portal couplings.

name: Indirect detection and late-decay energy injection
mass_range: masses in MeV-GeV range can inject photons/electrons if long-lived populations exist
observable: late-time decay or annihilation power into visible final states
required_observables: relic abundance; lifetime; annihilation cross sections; branching fractions; cosmological history
missing_observables: relic abundance; lifetimes; branching fractions; annihilation rates
tested_subregion: full 1sigma region only if long-lived abundance exists
applicability_notes: No indirect bound can be applied without a population of long-lived particles.

name: Perturbativity and tree-level unitarity of dark gauge sector
observable: g_D, alpha_D, beta_lambda, loop expansion size
available_observables: g_D 1sigma; beta_lambda derived from validated convention
applicability_notes: Passes a conservative perturbativity check; this is a validity classification, not an experimental exclusion.

name: Gauge anomaly and degree-of-freedom consistency
observable: absence of uncancelled gauge anomalies and consistent SSB degree counts
available_observables: validated model has no new chiral fermions; charge-one scalar Higgses U(1)_D
applicability_notes: No anomaly obstruction identified upstream; direct theory check passes.

name: FOPT/PTA theory-validity caveats: percolation, gauge dependence, and wall dynamics
mass_range: PTArcade 1sigma maps to sub-GeV masses; PTA best points have strong supercooling and large alpha
coupling_range: g_D and v known; wall friction parameters absent
observable: percolation probability, gauge-independent effective potential, wall velocity/runaway condition, bulk-flow template validity
required_observables: posterior-level FOPT outputs; wall friction; gauge-independent calculation; reheating/percolation checks
available_observables: semi-analytic alpha, beta_H, Tn/Tp/Treh machinery; PTA warning flags
missing_observables: wall-friction/runaway calculation; gauge-independent thermal potential; posterior distribution of percolation/reheating observables
tested_subregion: entire 1sigma region, especially supercooled points selected by PTArcade
source_title: Gauge-independent gravitational waves from a minimal dark U(1) sector with viable dark matter candidates
applicability_notes: Existing backend gives an approximate viability screen; not a first-principles exclusion or validation.
